name: Latvian Soviet Socialist Republic (1940–1953)

colors.symbolism: the hammer and sickle and the inscription

description: A plain red field with a gold hammer and sickle in the upper hoist, surmounted by the gold serif Latin initials 'LPSR' (Latvijas Padomju Sociālistiskā Republika).
history: Adopted on 25 August 1940 following the Soviet annexation of Latvia, this was the standard Soviet red banner with the republic's gold initials over a hammer and sickle. It was replaced by the wave-pattern flag adopted on 17 January 1953.

features.symbolism: the union of industrial workers and peasants
the name of the Latvian Soviet Socialist Republic
